argument: NVDA carries the basket's highest composite score at 15.283 with 17.36% 3-month momentum and an RSI of 58.5 -- strong trend without overbought exhaustion, leaving room for continuation. The HOLD synthesis leans on backward-looking insider sales and macro/geopolitical overhangs that are already discounted in price, while the forward setup -- AI capex cycle, datacenter GPU demand, and accelerating compute spend across hyperscalers -- continues to anchor earnings revisions higher. Insider selling at this stage of a secular up-cycle is routine portfolio rebalancing, not a fundamental signal; geopolitical export-control risk has been live for 2+ years and NVDA has consistently routed around it. The price-action vote (highest composite, healthy RSI, double-digit momentum) is the more current and probability-weighted evidence -- fade the qualitative HOLD and stay long.
